headlineSummary: HN sentiment is mixed: Anthropic drew both praise for lower-cost higher-level work and criticism for hallucinations [E1][E2], while Microsoft Copilot faced a pricing complaint with limited evidence [E3].

ranking.summary: Microsoft Copilot skews neutral-to-negative on limited evidence, driven by a pricing complaint that mocks the Copilot multiplier increase and Microsoft’s margins [E3].
Anthropic is mixed, with one comment praising Claude as higher-level work at a fraction of the cost and another criticizing Claude Code/Opus for hallucinations and unusable output [E1][E2].

evidence.excerpt: Anything I can do with the Claude stack is on higher level at a fraction of the cost. And as long as it works and looks good those business don't give a ** about unique design and scalable software engineering.

annotations.rationale: The commenter strongly praises the Claude stack as delivering higher-quality results at much lower cost.

evidence.excerpt: I had Claude code with opus building boards and using spice simulations. It completely hallucinated the capabilities of the board and made some pretty crazy claims like I had just stumbled onto the secret hardware billion dollar project that every home needed. None of the boards worked and I had to just do the project in codex. Opus seemed too busy congratulating itself to realize it produced gibberish.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Claude Code/Opus hallucinated, made crazy claims, and produced non-working results described as gibberish.

evidence.excerpt: The "multiplier" on Github Copilot went from 3 to 7.5. Nice to see that it is actually only 20-30% and Microsoft wanting to lose money slightly slower.

annotations.rationale: The commenter criticizes the Copilot pricing multiplier increase and sarcastically frames Microsoft as just trying to reduce losses.
